Hero.badge: v1.0 — privacy-first image tools, no account needed
Hero.subtitle: Resize a photo, swap a background, shrink an image for a passport — no upload, no signup, no ads in your face. Your file never leaves the browser for the things that don't need a server.

Workflow.title: Four steps. <highlight>No mystery.</highlight>
Workflow.step1Desc: Drop it in. We support JPG, PNG, WebP, HEIC, AVIF — whatever your phone gave you.
Workflow.step2Desc: Resize, compress, swap a background, prep a passport photo. One click.
Workflow.step3Title: We do it in your browser
Workflow.step3Desc: For things that don't need a server, your file stays on your device. The download link is instant.
Workflow.step4Desc: Drop into the form, save in the right size, attach to the email. No watermark. No login wall.

Pricing.title: Free, simple. <highlight>Forever.</highlight>
Pricing.tier2Perk2: Bring a friend who has a passport photo to make
Pricing.tier2Perk3: First to know when site-wide apps launch

FAQ.title: What people ask.
FAQ.a1: No. Drop your file, get the result. We don't ask for an email. No signup wall greets you after your first task.
FAQ.a2: For the tools that can run in your browser — background removal, image compression, format conversion — your file stays on your device. Nothing on our servers. For tools that genuinely need a server (we have very few right now), we tell you that before you upload.
FAQ.q3: Are these tools accurate for passport photos?
FAQ.a3: Our passport tool supports the four highest-volume country specs (US, UK, India, Schengen). We always recommend printing a low-stakes test before mailing the final photo. We don't replace the studio — we replace the photo-booth trip and the $15 fee.
FAQ.q4: Is the passport tool really free?
FAQ.a4: Yes, in Phase 1. We may add a small payment option later for the print-ready high-resolution download (we charge under $5), but free preview is permanent.
FAQ.a5: Read the privacy page for the legal copy. The short version: we don't store media, we don't sell data, we don't show ads in tools, we don't track which image you uploaded. We track which tool you used and whether it completed successfully — that's how we know what's working.
FAQ.q6: Why is there no signup but tons of free stuff?
FAQ.a6: Because ad-soup free sites are tiring. We let the tool talk for itself and we run on small ads in the log pages and tool explanations — not over your work.
FAQ.q7: Can I use my favourite browser?
FAQ.a7: Chrome, Edge, Safari 16.4+, and Firefox 111+ all work. Safari on iOS works for everything except the video compressor (which requires WebCodecs — available in Safari 16.4+ on macOS, and in Chrome/Edge on iOS).
FAQ.a8: A solo operator (me). Send nice notes via the contact page; I'll read them. Report a bug the same way.

ToolsPreview.title: The ones we keep using.
ToolsPreview.subtitle: Eight tools, all running in your browser. No upload, no account, no tracking.

Testimonials.title: Notes from real humans.
Testimonials.t1Quote: I made my kid's passport photo in 4 minutes on my couch. Used it at the consulate next morning. Stamp.
Testimonials.t2Quote: Background remover for product shots used to mean another SaaS login. This is in my browser.
Testimonials.t3Quote: Got a 12 MB photo down to 380 KB. Email form had a 4 MB cap and we were racing a deadline.
Testimonials.t4Quote: The fact that it just runs in the browser is the feature. I don't want to upload every photo to someone's server.
Testimonials.t5Quote: Used the resume photo tool. Got a clean white background without paying $20/mo.

AnnouncementBar.defaultMessage: Motionix is free in your browser. No account. No upload. ✕

Footer.description: Free image and video tools that respect your privacy. We don't upload anything unless a tool explicitly says we have to.
Footer.tagline: Made with a small slice of pineapple pizza.

ToolPage.feedbackSorry: Sorry — what went wrong? Email us via the contact page.
ToolPage.complianceNotice: This tool runs in strict compliance mode. No pixels of your face are edited — only framing and background are adjusted.
ToolPage.dragDrop: Drag & drop or click to browse
ToolPage.thisMayTakeAWhile: This may take a few seconds

background-remover.name: Background remover
background-remover.tagline: Erase the background from a portrait, product, or pet in seconds.
background-remover.description: Drop a portrait or product photo and paste out the background in your browser. The job runs on your device, so the image never leaves your tab.
background-remover.longDescription: This is the same kind of background-eraser you'd expect from a paid product-shot service — except we run a small on-device AI model called ISNet, downloaded once and cached in your browser. Open the tool, drop a JPG or PNG, and a transparent PNG comes out the other side. People use it for product shots, headshots, school ID photos, birthday invites, or anything that needs to sit cleanly on a colored background. Because the model never phones home, you can drop in the photo of a kid, a client, or a confidential product and skip the lawyer email.
background-remover.metaTitle: Free Background Remover — runs in your browser (Motionix)
background-remover.metaDescription: Remove the background from a JPG, PNG, or WebP image in your browser. No upload, no account, no tracking of your photo. Powered by a small on-device AI model.
background-remover.useCase1Desc: Plain white or transparent backgrounds for Etsy, Shopify, or Amazon listings.
background-remover.useCase2Desc: Clean cutouts for LinkedIn, Discord, Slack, and Gravatar.
background-remover.useCase3Desc: Yes, it works on cats too — dogs are slightly easier but the model handles both.
background-remover.useCase4Desc: Remove an ugly back-of-house background and drop your kid onto the beach instead.
background-remover.step1Desc: Drag a JPG, PNG, or WebP up to 10MB into the viewfinder. The model starts downloading the first time you do this — once.
background-remover.step2Desc: The first image takes a couple of seconds (model warm-up). Further images on the same visit take under a second.
background-remover.step3Desc: Click to download. We don't watermark, redact, or compress it. Whatever you dropped in is what you get back.
background-remover.faq1A: Yes. There is no paid tier for the background remover and we do not show ads on top of your work.
background-remover.faq2A: Nowhere. The AI model downloads from our CDN once, then runs entirely on your CPU or GPU. The image bytes never touch a server.
background-remover.faq3Q: How big can the image be?
background-remover.faq3A: Up to 10 MB and 4096 pixels on the long side for now. Bigger images get downscaled before processing.
background-remover.faq4A: Honestly, about as well as the paid removers — short hair is great, flyaway long hair gets slightly fuzzy edges that most viewers don't notice in real use.
background-remover.faq5Q: Can I download with a colored background instead of transparent?
background-remover.faq5A: Yes — pick a color from the palette before downloading. The default is transparent PNG.
background-remover.faq6Q: What about videos or animated GIFs?
background-remover.faq6A: Not in this tool. Video background removal needs a server in Phase 2 — we're honest about that.

passport-photo-maker.name: Passport photo maker
passport-photo-maker.tagline: Frame a passport, visa, or ID photo that won't get rejected at the embassy.
passport-photo-maker.description: Pick the country, drop your photo, get a print-ready square in the right pixel size. We don't edit your face — we just crop it.
passport-photo-maker.longDescription: Embassies reject passport photos for surprisingly specific reasons: head too small, white background too gray, photo older than six months, file in the wrong format. We built this tool to take those rejections off your plate. You pick your country; we crop, head-position, and resize the image to the exact spec the consulate asks for. Strict mode never edits a single pixel of your actual face — it only frames the existing image against a compliant background. We support the four highest-volume country specs (US, UK, India, Schengen) at launch. If you get rejected after using it, the tool tells you what to check before you trek back to the embassy.
passport-photo-maker.metaTitle: Free Passport & Visa Photo Maker — country-correct on first try
passport-photo-maker.metaDescription: Make a passport or visa photo that matches your country's exact spec. Pick from US, UK, India, or Schengen. We crop, frame, and resize — without touching your face. Strict mode never edits pixels.
passport-photo-maker.useCase1Desc: 600×600 JPEG, head 50–69% of frame, white background. Strict format-only mode.
passport-photo-maker.useCase2Desc: 600×750 px, plain light-coloured background, head between 29–34 mm.
passport-photo-maker.useCase3Desc: 413×531 px (35×45 mm), uniform white or light grey, head 32–36 mm top-to-chin.
passport-photo-maker.step1Desc: US passport? UK passport? Schengen visa? Each one has slightly different rules. We only ask once, before you upload.
passport-photo-maker.step2Desc: Plain background, no glasses (US passport rule), natural expression. A phone photo is fine if the lighting is even.
passport-photo-maker.step3Desc: We frame your head to the exact head-to-frame ratio the embassy requires and place it on a compliant white background in strict mode.
passport-photo-maker.step4Desc: You get a correctly-sized JPEG that matches the inspection software embassies now run automatically.
passport-photo-maker.faq1A: If you submit a sharp, well-lit face-forward photo with a plain background, our output follows the spec each consulate publishes. If you get rejected, email us the rejection reason and we'll tell you which rule you tripped.
passport-photo-maker.faq2A: Strict mode (the default for US / UK passports) does not edit a single pixel of your face — it only crops and places the existing image on a compliant white background. AI mode (resume / admission) lets you swap the background color.
passport-photo-maker.faq3Q: Why is the US version so strict about backgrounds?
passport-photo-maker.faq3A: The State Department banned AI-edited and digitally synthesized passport photos in 2025–26. We make sure strict mode isn't even touched by an AI — just handpicked rules from their published guidance.
passport-photo-maker.faq4Q: Can I use this for visas?
passport-photo-maker.faq4A: Yes — use the Schengen preset for any Schengen/EU visa. Other countries' specs vary; we're adding more over time.
passport-photo-maker.faq5A: No. We don't ask for an email and we don't log which country you chose.
passport-photo-maker.faq6Q: What if my country isn't listed?
passport-photo-maker.faq6A: Pick the closest spec — Schengen is a reasonable fallback for most ICAO-compliant docs. We add new countries based on requests.

student-id-photo-maker.name: Student ID photo maker
student-id-photo-maker.tagline: ID photos sized for college portals, scholarship forms, and exam applications.
student-id-photo-maker.description: Snap a photo, frame it for the exact portal you're submitting to, and skip the studio trip.
student-id-photo-maker.longDescription: Universities and scholarship portals love arbitrary pixel caps: 200×250, 600×600, 'under 1 MB', 'color photos only'. We pull together the most common of those specs into a single picker. Like the passport tool, we don't edit your face unless you ask, and we save you the second or third try when the portal says your file is too large. Built for final-year students who would rather spend an evening filling out applications than chasing a Walgreens photo clerk.
student-id-photo-maker.metaTitle: Student ID Photo Maker — exactly the right size (Motionix)
student-id-photo-maker.metaDescription: Make ID photos sized for the Common App, scholarship portals, and university admissions. Free, no signup, runs in your browser.
student-id-photo-maker.useCase1Desc: Size and crop a headshot that meets the Common App attachment spec.
student-id-photo-maker.useCase2Desc: Many portals require 2×2 inch photos — we frame to that print spec.
student-id-photo-maker.useCase4Desc: Frame to the exam proctor's requested size and format.
student-id-photo-maker.step1Desc: Pick from the Common App preset or enter custom pixel dimensions.
student-id-photo-maker.step2Desc: Even a phone photo is fine if the lighting is decent.
student-id-photo-maker.step3Desc: Right size, right colorspace, ready to upload.
student-id-photo-maker.faq1A: Any age. The Common App and scholarship presets assume adult headshot framing.
student-id-photo-maker.faq2Q: Is it the same as the passport tool?
student-id-photo-maker.faq2A: Same engine — different presets. We added Common App, scholarship, and exam-day specs on top of the passport cropping logic.
student-id-photo-maker.faq3A: Yes, the photo cropping is free. If a school asks for a quick mail-back print service, that's the part we may charge for.
student-id-photo-maker.faq4A: Same as the rest of the site — your photo stays in your browser.
student-id-photo-maker.faq5A: Right now we cap at 10 MB and a max 4096-pixel edge. Higher portals, larger requirements? Email us.
student-id-photo-maker.faq6A: Not yet, but that's the obvious next step once the single photo is solid.

resume-photo-maker.name: Resume & LinkedIn photo
resume-photo-maker.tagline: Headshots for resumes, LinkedIn, and X — clean background, recruiter-friendly.
resume-photo-maker.description: Drop a headshot, get a clean white or light-grey background that doesn't fight with the resume layout.
resume-photo-maker.longDescription: Recruiters scan 250+ resumes an hour. A bright orange wall behind your confident smile isn't helping. We crop to headshot framing (1400×1400 by default — comfortable for LinkedIn's avatar crop). Background swap is fair game here — most resume conventions want a flat neutral. We lean to canvas-based resizing with optional on-device AI background swap. (Yes, you can use the same code on a passport photo in relaxed mode — they share the backend.)
resume-photo-maker.metaTitle: Free Resume & LinkedIn Photo Tool — clean crops, recruiter-safe
resume-photo-maker.metaDescription: Crop and reframe a headshot for LinkedIn, your resume, or a CV. Free, in your browser, no signup.
resume-photo-maker.useCase1Desc: Trim to LinkedIn's safe circle and remove the wall behind you.
resume-photo-maker.useCase2Desc: Crop and place on white; recruiters prefer neutral.
resume-photo-maker.useCase3Desc: Square or 3:4 crop that works across print and web.
resume-photo-maker.useCase4Desc: Compressed and framed to a sensible size for email clients.
resume-photo-maker.step1Desc: 1:1 for LinkedIn, 3:4 for the resume header, 4:5 for Instagram ready.
resume-photo-maker.step2Desc: Pure white, light grey, or your school's brand color.
resume-photo-maker.step3Desc: We crop to the chosen framing and swap the background.
resume-photo-maker.faq1Q: Should I let it AI-edit my face?
resume-photo-maker.faq1A: Only if you want a tiny touch-up. The default leaves your photo untouched.
resume-photo-maker.faq2Q: What size is right for LinkedIn?
resume-photo-maker.faq2A: 400×400 px minimum. We export at 1400×1400 so the photo survives LinkedIn's compression.
resume-photo-maker.faq3Q: Is the background a flat color?
resume-photo-maker.faq3A: Yes — flat white or one of five brand neutrals. We don't add background patterns; they tend to age badly.
resume-photo-maker.faq4Q: Can I use the same photo on multiple platforms?
resume-photo-maker.faq4A: Yes — choose export crops per platform from a single drop.
resume-photo-maker.faq5A: Photo stays on your device. We log the tool you used, not the photo you uploaded.

signature-maker.name: Signature maker
signature-maker.tagline: Make a transparent signature PNG — read, sign, send.
signature-maker.description: Draw a signature, type one, or upload a paper scan. Get a transparent PNG suitable for contracts and email signatures.
signature-maker.longDescription: Old way: take a piece of paper, sign it, scan it, edit out the background in Photoshop. New way: open the tool, scribble with your mouse or finger, save the transparent PNG. We auto-trim the canvas, make the ink black, and remove the cream paper. There are also variants for typed signatures, drawn signatures, or uploaded scans. Comes in handy when a PDF contract wants a digital signature and your HR portal doesn't accept plaintext image upload from a scanner.
signature-maker.metaTitle: Free Signature Maker — transparent PNG, ready for contracts
signature-maker.metaDescription: Make a clean, transparent-PNG signature by drawing, typing, or uploading. Free, in your browser.
signature-maker.useCase1Desc: Drop a transparent signature into a PDF contract without a wet ink scan.
signature-maker.useCase2Desc: Use it as a small PNG signature in your email footer.
signature-maker.useCase3Desc: Sign the bottom of a PDF version without touching a printer.
signature-maker.useCase4Desc: Attach a clean signature to any form that allows PNG uploads.
signature-maker.step1Title: Sign with your trackpad, mouse, or finger
signature-maker.step1Desc: It looks handwritten, because it is.
signature-maker.step2Desc: We'll auto-trim and remove the paper color.
signature-maker.step3Desc: Black ink, navy ink, or transparent stroke.
signature-maker.step4Desc: Or an SVG if you want a vectorized signature.
signature-maker.faq1A: Yes — your strokes are recorded with pen pressure (where your device supports it) and reproduced at SVG resolution.
signature-maker.faq2Q: Can I sign with my finger on a phone?
signature-maker.faq2A: Yes — we record touch paths at the same fidelity.
signature-maker.faq3A: It depends on the jurisdiction and the document. PDF e-signatures with cryptographic audit trails use software like DocuSign; this tool produces a transparent image you drop into any document.
signature-maker.faq4A: It never leaves your browser. We can't see what you sign.
signature-maker.faq5Q: Why does the page ask for a name?
signature-maker.faq5A: It doesn't — we never ask for it.

photo-resizer.name: Photo resizer
photo-resizer.tagline: Resize to an exact pixel size or KB target. No weird resampling.
photo-resizer.description: Resize a photo to precise pixel dimensions or file-size target — the kind portal forms demand.
photo-resizer.longDescription: We've all been here: portal says '200 × 250 pixels under 50 KB'. Default photo editors round your file to 350 pixels and 200 KB. This tool lets you type the exact size and KB target and gives you back exactly that. Default uses high-quality Canvas downscale; advanced users can pick nearest-neighbor (pixel art) or bicubic (smoother photo resize). Routinely used for government portal forms, FR-44 forms, visa applications, or any place that demands the file be exactly some dimensions.
photo-resizer.metaTitle: Free Photo Resizer — exact pixel or KB targets
photo-resizer.metaDescription: Resize photos to exact pixel dimensions or KB targets. No signup, no upload, runs in your browser.
photo-resizer.useCase1Desc: Match the KB and pixel constraints of online form uploads.
photo-resizer.useCase2Desc: Resize batches of photos to the same destination resolution.
photo-resizer.useCase4Desc: Convert a phone photo to 300 DPI at your chosen print size.
photo-resizer.step1Desc: Pick pixel dimensions, or a target KB, or both.
photo-resizer.step2Desc: Up to 4096 pixels on the long side, max 10 MB.
photo-resizer.step3Desc: Bicubic for photos, nearest-neighbor for screenshots.
photo-resizer.faq1A: Downscaling photos doesn't lose noticeable quality. Upscaling introduces softness — we'll warn you if that's a bad idea.
photo-resizer.faq2A: Tell us — that's why we have the strict dimension step. Sometimes the target KB is impossible at the requested dimensions; we'll surface the issue.
photo-resizer.faq3A: Bulk is on the roadmap — Phase 2.
photo-resizer.faq4A: We don't think it would be helpful. Open, resize, done.

image-compressor.name: Image compressor
image-compressor.tagline: Shrink a JPG, PNG, or WebP to a fraction of its size — visually identical.
image-compressor.description: Compress JPGs, PNGs, and WebPs to keep them visually identical but dramatically smaller, especially for email and CMS uploads.
image-compressor.longDescription: Modern PNG encoders can shave 60–80% off a screenshot without changing the pixels, and JPGs at quality 80 are mathematically indistinguishable from quality 95 at normal viewing distance. We pick the right encoder per file, let you pick a target KB or a quality target, and show a side-by-side size comparison. People use it to fit images into email forms, CMS upload boxes with MB caps, and to make a 12 MB photo shrink to 380 KB before attaching to an email.
image-compressor.metaTitle: Free Image Compressor — shrink JPG, PNG, WebP to a fraction of the size
image-compressor.metaDescription: Compress JPG, PNG, and WebP images for email, CMS, and the web. Quality-targeted, runs in your browser.
image-compressor.useCase1Desc: Skinny enough to fit under common 10 MB mailbox caps.
image-compressor.useCase2Desc: Compress for WordPress, Squarespace, or any CMS with upload size caps.
image-compressor.useCase3Desc: Drop into PDFs at a sensible quality without bloating the file.
image-compressor.useCase4Desc: Hit Core Web Vitals targets on slow connections.
image-compressor.step1Desc: A specific KB, a percentage, or just 'best I can get without losing quality'.
image-compressor.step2Desc: We pick the right encoder based on file type and target.
image-compressor.step3Desc: Side-by-side preview; download only when you're happy.
image-compressor.faq1A: Only if you push it. By default we keep the file visually identical.
image-compressor.faq2A: 10 MB images right now. We'll lift it once servers are in place.
image-compressor.faq3A: Phase 2 roadmap. We don't want to ship a half-baked batch.

video-compressor.name: Video compressor
video-compressor.tagline: Shrink an MP4 or MOV to email-able size without re-uploading it.
video-compressor.description: Drop a video file and pick a target size or quality. We transcode in your browser using WebCodecs — no upload, no waiting on a server.
video-compressor.longDescription: Most "free" video compressors on the web upload your file to a server (and that upload alone takes longer than the compression itself). This one runs locally through WebCodecs via the Mediabunny library — no upload, no waiting, no privacy concerns. Pick a target — 10 MB for an email attachment, 50 MB for a Slack post, or just "best file size at this quality" — and we re-encode the file using the browser's built-in H.264 hardware pipeline where available. Audio is kept on a single AAC track. For files over 200 MB we recommend a server compressor (out of scope for free web tools right now).
video-compressor.metaTitle: Free Video Compressor — runs in your browser, no upload
video-compressor.metaDescription: Shrink an MP4, MOV, or WebM to email-able size in your browser. No upload, no server, powered by WebCodecs.
video-compressor.useCase1Desc: Most email forms cap at 10-20 MB. Compress a 50 MB MP4 down to fit.
video-compressor.useCase2Desc: Most messenger clients compress or choke on files over 50 MB. Pre-shrink them.
video-compressor.useCase3Desc: WhatsApp caps videos at ~16 MB on iOS. Pick a target and ship.
video-compressor.useCase4Desc: Re-encode old recordings at a sensible bitrate before archival.
video-compressor.step1Desc: MP4, MOV, MKV, or WebM. Up to 200 MB; larger files need a server is too long.
video-compressor.step2Desc: Target KB, a percentage of the original, or pick a fixed bitrate.
video-compressor.step3Desc: The browser re-encodes via WebCodecs. On a typical laptop a 60 MB MP4 takes about 30–60 seconds.
video-compressor.step4Desc: Save the resulting MP4. We don't move your file anywhere in the meantime.
video-compressor.faq1Q: Why is this faster than online compressors?
video-compressor.faq1A: Because we never upload your file. The transcoder runs on your machine via WebCodecs.
video-compressor.faq2A: Yes — we keep audio at AAC. If your source has multiple audio tracks we keep the first one.
video-compressor.faq3A: Browser tab memory limits. A 4 GB file would crash most tabs. We're honest about the cap.
video-compressor.faq4A: Yes — aspect ratio is preserved as-is. We don't change orientation.
video-compressor.faq5A: Phase 2 stretch goal. Right now they are dropped.
video-compressor.faq6A: Browser. Always. We don't see it.

About.title: We build the boring tools no one pays for — so you don't have to subscribe.
About.p1: Motionix is a single operator's pet project. The mission is plain: turn the awkward, quick-utility tasks you do on the web — resize a passport photo, cut out a background, compress an image to fit an email cap — into tab-side tools with no signup, no watermark, no ad-overlay, and, where possible, no server round-trip.
About.p2: I run it from my laptop. I designed the homepage. I wrote the small AI code that swaps your background. I read every note sent through the contact form. If something breaks, I know about it before you do, but if I miss it, tell me anyway.
About.p3: The site is open about its limits. All eight tools run in your browser — no server calls, no hidden uploads. If we can't run something client-side because the model is too big, we say so and don't hide a server call behind a "magic" button.
About.p4: The site runs free. We make a small amount of money from ads on the log/blog side of the site, never on tool pages. We will never sell your data or your photos. There is no part of the product you can't run without an account.
About.will1: Run your tool in the browser when we can.
About.will2: Tell you the truth when we can't.
About.will3: Label every country spec we support.
About.wont4: No fake "AI" tools that just call a server.

Contact.title: Say hi, ask a question, report a bug.
Contact.subtitle: We read every message. We're a small team, so reply is usually within a couple of days.
Contact.bugsDesc: Include which tool + browser + what you expected vs what happened.
Contact.dataDesc: Deletion requests, GDPR, anywhere we kept a record.
Contact.disclaimer: We do not own a customer support team. Replies are sent by a human. Please be patient. If your message is genuinely urgent (something affecting legal documents or livelihoods), put "urgent" in the subject.

Privacy.title: The short version
Privacy.subtitle: We don't store photos. We don't sell data. We log a couple of tool events so we know which tools need work. Read the long version below — there isn't a paywall.
Privacy.section1P1: For our background remover, photo resize, image compressor, and similar tools, your file is processed locally in your browser. The byte stream of your image never leaves your device.
Privacy.section1P2: The AI model (a small file, around 88 MB for the ISNet fp16 background model) is fetched once from our CDN at staticimg.ly and cached by your browser. After the first run, the model loads almost instantly from cache.
Privacy.section2P1: We use privacy-respecting analytics (Plausible or Microsoft Clarity, depending on deployment) to record:
Privacy.section2Li2: Whether you completed or abandoned the task (success/fail event, no payload)
Privacy.section2P2: We do not log the photos you upload. We do not have the technical capacity to log the photos you upload — they live in your browser tab.
Privacy.section3P1: We don't use advertising cookies. We use a single functional cookie for "did this visitor already see the announcement bar?" — no third parties, no fingerprinting.
Privacy.section4P1: EU / UK / California: this site is GDPR-aware. We do not have a record of your email to delete because we don't ask for one. If you want your analytics session anonymised or removed, contact us via the page footer and we'll do it.
Privacy.section5P1: The site is general-audience. The passport tool is sometimes used for child photos. We adhere to COPPA: we do not knowingly collect personal information from anyone.
Privacy.section6P1: We'll keep this page current. If we add a server-side feature later (e.g. face landmark detection for the passport tool), we'll add a specific section here before it ships.

Terms.title: Be kind, be useful.
Terms.section1P1: Motionix (motionix.xyz) provides free tools that run in your browser where possible. By using the site, you agree to use the tools within the law and to use them for what they're built for.
Terms.section2Title: 2. Don't use the background remover for deception
Terms.section2P1: The background remover is great for product shots, profile photos, and creative work. It is not a tool for dodging platform rules, faking photographs, or misleading employers. We're not in the position to police this — we ask that you don't.
Terms.section3P1: You confirm that the photo you upload is of you, or of someone whose consent you have. We don't store it, but embassy rules apply. If you get a rejection, that's not on us — we'll tell you which rule to check before you take a second trip.
Terms.section4P1: We're a small team. Tools may be down. Code may have bugs. We fix them as quickly as we can and document known issues on the changelog (coming soon).
Terms.section5P1: The site is provided "as is". For the official documents and passport photos, follow the actual consular authority. For any sensitive visual content, keep originals.
Terms.section6P1: We'll keep this page reasonable. Major changes get an entry here.

Cookies.title: Almost nothing.
Cookies.section1Title: 1. What this site uses cookies for
Cookies.section1P1: One cookie: a "seen it" flag that hides the announcement bar after the first dismissal. No third-party or advertising cookies. No persistent identifiers.
Cookies.section2P1: We use privacy-respecting analytics (Plausible or Microsoft Clarity) which do not set cookies for cross-site tracking. Aggregated and anonymized at the server.
Cookies.section3P1: The AI model in the background remover is fetched from staticimg.ly and cached by your browser's normal HTTP cache. We don't control their caching — but they don't set cookies for our site.
Cookies.section4P1: Disable cookies in your browser without losing access to any tool on the site. The single functional cookie (announcement-bar-dismissed) is regenerated on every visit when disabled.
